title: Scaevola plumieri
intro: Scaevola plumieri, also known as sea lavender or fairy fan flower, is a flowering plant native to tropical regions of the Pacific Ocean. The health benefits of Scaevola plumieri include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Scaevola plumieri is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which are commonly used to treat skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Scaevola plumieri is classified within the family Goodeniaceae, which is a group of flowering plants native to Australia, New Zealand, and the Pacific Islands. This family is part of the larger order Asterales, a group of flowering plants that also includes sunflowers and daisies. Within the Goodeniaceae family, Scaevola plumieri is further categorized as a member of the genus Scaevola, which comprises around 60 species of flowering plants. The genus Scaevola is closely related to the genera Goodenia and Suteria, and all are part of the larger clade Goodenia. Scaevola plumieri is a specific species within this complex classification system.
